attacking_style: Long-range diesel attacks from established breakaways, typically 25km+ from finish. Thrives on technical parcours combining climbs and descents where sustained power overwhelms pure climbers.
team_tactics: Often deployed as breakaway specialist rather than pure domestique. Executes high-speed leadout work when called upon (71 km/h recorded), then given freedom to hunt stages from breaks.
terrain_preference: Punchy, technical courses with repeated climbs and descents. Excels on short TT courses with power demands. Cobbled climbing finishes suit diesel engine. Technical descents expose weaknesses versus pure descenders.
vulnerability: Sprinter teams instantly neutralize breakaway attempts regardless of terrain difficulty. Peak timing issues evident (ITT form not translating to stage race performance). Technical descents reveal weakness versus specialists like Dennis.
key_patterns: Attacks solo from established breakaways 25km+ out, not from peloton (3 observations)
Exceptional recovery ability: delivers 'ride of day' performances on consecutive hard stages (2 observations)
Top-tier short TT specialist on punchy courses, French champion level (2 observations)
Poor tactical reading: attacks immediately after being caught, wastes energy against peloton mood (2 observations)
Diesel power engine sustains high speeds on technical terrain where climbers fade (3 observations)
form_trajectory: negative: 2023 observation shows poor race-reading and wasted efforts. Earlier peak timing issues (2021 Giro disappointment despite ITT win) suggest ongoing challenges converting fitness to results.
